- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- vercel-react-best-practices: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering. This skill should be used when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code to ensure optimal performance patterns. Triggers on tasks involving React components, Next.js pages, data fetching, bundle optimization, or performance improvements.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Type check passes. Both fixes are in [app/pricing-profile/new/_components/AdjustmentControls.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/AdjustmentControls.tsx):

- **Select label** (line 78-80): `SelectValue` now uses base-ui's render-prop form to map `"globalWholesale"` → "Global Wholesale Price" in the trigger.
- **Adjustment input** (lines 40-70, 120-129): Switched to `type="text"` with `inputMode="decimal"`, backed by local string state (`text`). The `handleChange` regex strips anything that isn't a digit or single decimal point — so negatives, letters, and `-` keypresses are all rejected. Empty/partial input (e.g. `""`, `"."`) is kept in the display while editing so backspace works naturally; `handleBlur` normalizes those back to `"0"` on focus loss. The form state still receives a valid non-negative number on every keystroke.

input.content:
# Add the ability to delete a saved profile

## Context

The Saved Profiles page ([app/profiles/page.tsx](app/profiles/page.tsx)) currently has no way to remove a profile once it's created — the only paths to the store mutate it via the "New profile" flow. The backend is already there: `DELETE /api/pricing-profiles/[id]` and `deleteProfile(id)` in the store both exist and work. This change wires up the UI: a trash-icon control on each card, a confirmation dialog, and a refresh of the list after deletion.

## Approach

A small client component (`DeleteProfileButton`) renders an icon-only ghost trash button in each card's `CardAction` slot. Clicking it opens an `AlertDialog` (newly installed via shadcn). Confirming calls the existing `DELETE` route, shows a sonner toast for success/failure, and runs `router.refresh()` so the server component re-fetches the profile list.

The page itself stays a server component — only the per-card action becomes a client island.

## Files to modify / create

1. **Install the dialog primitive**
   - Run `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add alert-dialog` (base-nova preset, same registry the existing primitives use).
   - This creates [components/ui/alert-dialog.tsx](components/ui/alert-dialog.tsx) and may add a small base-ui dialog dependency if not present.

2. **Create [app/profiles/_components/DeleteProfileButton.tsx](app/profiles/_components/DeleteProfileButton.tsx)** (new, `"use client"`):
   - Props: `{ profileId: string; profileName: string }`.
   - Uses `useTransition` + `fetch` + sonner toasts — same pattern as [app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SaveAsDraftButton.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SaveAsDraftButton.tsx).
   - Renders `<AlertDialogTrigger asChild>` wrapping a ghost-variant icon `Button` with `<Trash2>` from `lucide-react` and an `aria-label`.
   - Dialog body names the profile being deleted; confirm button uses `variant="destructive"`.
   - On confirm: `await fetch(\`/api/pricing-profiles/\${profileId}\`, { method: "DELETE" })` → on `res.ok` (204) call `router.refresh()` from `next/navigation` and `toast.success(...)`; on failure `toast.error(...)`.

3. **Edit [app/profiles/page.tsx](app/profiles/page.tsx)**:
   - Import `DeleteProfileButton` and `CardAction` from [components/ui/card.tsx](components/ui/card.tsx).
   - Inside each `<CardHeader>` (after `CardTitle`/`CardDescription`), add:
     ```tsx
     <CardAction>
       <DeleteProfileButton profileId={profile.id} profileName={profile.name} />
     </CardAction>
     ```
   - `CardAction` already has `col-start-2 row-span-2 self-start justify-self-end` styling, so it lands top-right without extra layout work.

## Things to reuse (don't recreate)

- `DELETE` handler: [app/api/pricing-profiles/[id]/route.ts](app/api/pricing-profiles/%5Bid%5D/route.ts) lines 35–40 (returns 204 / 404).
- `deleteProfile(id)`: [lib/db/store.ts:120](lib/db/store.ts#L120).
- Client-mutation pattern: [app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SaveAsDraftButton.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SaveAsDraftButton.tsx) — `useTransition` + `fetch` + sonner toasts.
- Toaster is already mounted (sonner exists at [components/ui/sonner.tsx](components/ui/sonner.tsx)).
- `Button` has the `destructive` variant available out of the box.
- `CardAction` slot is already styled for top-right placement in the card header grid.

## Out of scope

- Undo / soft-delete. The store hard-deletes by design.
- Bulk delete or multi-select.
- Cascade prompts ("this profile is applied to X customers — continue?") — the in-memory store doesn't track reverse relations, and the brief's resolver simply skips missing profiles.

## Verification

1. `pnpm tsc --noEmit` — type-clean.
2. `pnpm dev`, navigate to `/profiles`.
3. Seed data ships three profiles. For each:
   - Click the trash icon → dialog opens, names the right profile.
   - Cancel → dialog closes, card remains.
   - Confirm → toast appears, card disappears without a full page reload (router.refresh re-runs the server component).
4. After deleting all three, the empty state in [app/profiles/page.tsx:50-65](app/profiles/page.tsx#L50-L65) renders.
5. Restart the dev server (in-memory store resets via seed) and confirm initial state returns.
6. Edge case: hit `DELETE /api/pricing-profiles/does-not-exist` via curl → 404 with `{ error: "not found" }`. Trigger the same path from the UI by deleting twice in rapid succession (second call after refresh should be a no-op since the button is gone) — defensive `toast.error` shows on any non-2xx.

# Add the ability to delete a saved profile

## Context

The Saved Profiles page ([app/profiles/page.tsx](app/profiles/page.tsx)) currently has no way to remove a profile once it's created — the only paths to the store mutate it via the "New profile" flow. The backend is already there: `DELETE /api/pricing-profiles/[id]` and `deleteProfile(id)` in the store both exist and work. This change wires up the UI: a trash-icon control on each card, a confirmation dialog, and a refresh of the list after deletion.

# Extend the mark-as-complete flow to all three profile cards ## Context The new-profile page ([app/pricing-profile/new/page.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/page.tsx)) currently has three cards stacked vertically. Only the first ([ProfileBasicCard](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/ProfileBasicCard.tsx)) supports the "mark as complete → collapse to summary, show Completed badge" pattern. The Products and Customers cards stay fully expanded the whole time, and the page header carries a placeholder "Cancel" and a "Save as Draft" button that fires regardless of how filled in the form is. This change makes the flow read like a checklist: each card has a **Mark as complete** action in its bottom-right; when completed, it collapses to a one-line summary. The page-level actions (**Cancel** and **Save and Publish**) move to the bottom of the page, with **Save and Publish** disabled until all three cards are complete. ## Approach 1. Extend the reducer with two new completion flags + matching actions and action-hooks (mirrors `basicCompleted` / `markBasicCompleted`). 2. Refactor [SelectProductPricingCard](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SelectProductPricingCard.tsx) and [AssignCustomersCard](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/AssignCustomersCard.tsx) to render two views (`completed` vs `editing`), copying the [ProfileBasicCard](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/ProfileBasicCard.tsx) skeleton: - Editing view: existing content + a primary **Mark as complete** button anchored bottom-right. - Completed view: a short summary line + a **Make Changes** ghost button (same as basic card). - Badge in the header flips from `● Not Started` (secondary) to `● Completed` (default). 3. Move the page footer actions: delete the `Cancel`/`SaveAsDraftButton` pair from the header, render `` at the bottom of the page below the cards. 4. Rename `SaveAsDraftButton` → `SaveAndPublishButton` (file + component) and gate `disabled` on `state.basicCompleted && state.productsCompleted && state.customersCompleted` in addition to the existing `pending`. 5. `Cancel` becomes a `` styled as `Button variant="outline"`. ## Files to modify / create **[app/pricing-profile/new/_components/ProfileFormProvider.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/ProfileFormProvider.tsx)** - Add `productsCompleted: boolean` and `customersCompleted: boolean` to `ProfileFormState`, default `false` in the initial state. - Extend the `Action` union with `{ type: "markProductsCompleted"; completed: boolean }` and `{ type: "markCustomersCompleted"; completed: boolean }`; add matching reducer cases. - Expose `markProductsCompleted` and `markCustomersCompleted` from `useProfileFormActions()` alongside the existing `markBasicCompleted`. - Edits that change inputs while the card is "completed" should NOT auto-flip the flag — match the basic card's behavior (user must explicitly click Make Changes). **[app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SelectProductPricingCard.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SelectProductPricingCard.tsx)** - Read `state.productsCompleted` and the new action. - Wrap current body in a `{completed ? : }` conditional. - Editor footer (replaces the current Back/Next scroll buttons): right-aligned **Mark as complete** button, `disabled` when `state.selectedProductIds.length === 0`. - Summary line: `${n} product(s) selected · ${formatAdjustment(state.adjustment)} (${mode})` — reuse `formatAdjustment` from [lib/pricing.ts](lib/pricing.ts). - Header gets the same badge pattern as ProfileBasicCard. **[app/pricing-profile/new/_components/AssignCustomersCard.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/AssignCustomersCard.tsx)** - Same shape. Editor footer: right-aligned **Mark as complete**, `disabled` when both selection arrays are empty. - Summary line: `${groupCount} group(s), ${customerCount} customer(s) assigned` (graceful pluralization). - Header badge mirrors the other cards. **[app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SaveAsDraftButton.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SaveAsDraftButton.tsx) → SaveAndPublishButton.tsx** - Rename file and exported component to `SaveAndPublishButton`. - Read `state` from `useProfileForm()` and add `const allComplete = state.basicCompleted && state.productsCompleted && state.customersCompleted` to the `disabled` prop. - Update button label: `pending ? "Publishing…" : "Save and Publish"`. - Toast success message likewise. **[app/pricing-profile/new/page.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/page.tsx)** - Remove the `Cancel` + `SaveAsDraftButton` pair from the header (keep the heading/description block on the left). - Below the cards container, add a footer row: ```tsx
``` ## Things to reuse (don't recreate) - The completion-flag-+-collapse pattern: copy verbatim from [ProfileBasicCard.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/ProfileBasicCard.tsx) (badge text + colors, Pencil icon, "Make Changes" outline button). - `formatAdjustment(adjustment)` from [lib/pricing.ts](lib/pricing.ts) for the products summary. - `useProfileForm()` / `useProfileFormActions()` from the provider — no new context needed. - `Button`, `Card`, `Badge`, `Link` primitives are all in place; no shadcn installs required. - Existing `useSaveProfile()` callback in the provider — the rename touches only the button wrapper, not the save logic. ## Out of scope - Validation beyond "non-empty selection" gates (no enforcement that adjustment values are sensible, no overlap-warning across profiles). - Auto-scroll between cards on mark-complete. The page already scrolls naturally; chasing the next card automatically is an upgrade for later. - Reverting on Cancel — the form state is component-local, so navigating away discards it without a confirm prompt. ## Verification 1. `pnpm tsc --noEmit` — type-clean (the new state fields and actions are all internal). 2. `pnpm build` — RSC boundary smoke test; the page tree shouldn't regress. 3. `pnpm dev`, navigate to `/pricing-profile/new`: - Header should NO LONGER show Cancel/Save. A new bottom action row should appear after the three cards. - Save and Publish should be disabled. Hover/inspect to confirm. - Fill in basic name → click **Mark as complete** → first card collapses, badge flips to "● Completed". Save button still disabled. - Select at least one product, set an adjustment → bottom-right of the products card has **Mark as complete** enabled → click it → card collapses to a one-line summary including product count and adjustment string. Save still disabled. - Tick at least one customer or group → bottom-right **Mark as complete** enabled → click → card collapses. **Save and Publish** now becomes enabled. - Click **Save and Publish** → button shows "Publishing…", toast appears, server reads the same payload as before. - For each completed card, click **Make Changes** → card re-expands with prior selections intact and the completion flag clears (Save and Publish disables again). - Click **Cancel** → routes to `/profiles`. 4. Hit `/profiles` and confirm the newly published profile appears in the list (the API contract didn't change).

# Extend the mark-as-complete flow to all three profile cards ## Context The new-profile page ([app/pricing-profile/new/page.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/page.tsx)) currently has three cards stacked vertically. Only the first ([ProfileBasicCard](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/ProfileBasicCard.tsx)) supports the "mark as complete → collapse to summary, show Completed badge" pattern. The Products and Customers cards stay fully expanded the whole time, and the page header carries a placeholder "Cancel" and a "Save as Draft" button that fires regardless of how filled in the form is. This change makes the flow read like a checklist: each card has a **Mark as complete** action in its bottom-right; when completed, it collapses to a one-line summary. The page-level actions (**Cancel** and **Save and Publish**) move to the bottom of the page, with **Save and Publish** disabled until all three cards are complete. ## Approach 1. Extend the reducer with two new completion flags + matching actions and action-hooks (mirrors `basicCompleted` / `markBasicCompleted`). 2. Refactor [SelectProductPricingCard](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SelectProductPricingCard.tsx) and [AssignCustomersCard](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/AssignCustomersCard.tsx) to render two views (`completed` vs `editing`), copying the [ProfileBasicCard](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/ProfileBasicCard.tsx) skeleton: - Editing view: existing content + a primary **Mark as complete** button anchored bottom-right. - Completed view: a short summary line + a **Make Changes** ghost button (same as basic card). - Badge in the header flips from `● Not Started` (secondary) to `● Completed` (default). 3. Move the page footer actions: delete the `Cancel`/`SaveAsDraftButton` pair from the header, render `` at the bottom of the page below the cards. 4. Rename `SaveAsDraftButton` → `SaveAndPublishButton` (file + component) and gate `disabled` on `state.basicCompleted && state.productsCompleted && state.customersCompleted` in addition to the existing `pending`. 5. `Cancel` becomes a `` styled as `Button variant="outline"`. ## Files to modify / create **[app/pricing-profile/new/_components/ProfileFormProvider.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/ProfileFormProvider.tsx)** - Add `productsCompleted: boolean` and `customersCompleted: boolean` to `ProfileFormState`, default `false` in the initial state. - Extend the `Action` union with `{ type: "markProductsCompleted"; completed: boolean }` and `{ type: "markCustomersCompleted"; completed: boolean }`; add matching reducer cases. - Expose `markProductsCompleted` and `markCustomersCompleted` from `useProfileFormActions()` alongside the existing `markBasicCompleted`. - Edits that change inputs while the card is "completed" should NOT auto-flip the flag — match the basic card's behavior (user must explicitly click Make Changes). **[app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SelectProductPricingCard.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SelectProductPricingCard.tsx)** - Read `state.productsCompleted` and the new action. - Wrap current body in a `{completed ? : }` conditional. - Editor footer (replaces the current Back/Next scroll buttons): right-aligned **Mark as complete** button, `disabled` when `state.selectedProductIds.length === 0`. - Summary line: `${n} product(s) selected · ${formatAdjustment(state.adjustment)} (${mode})` — reuse `formatAdjustment` from [lib/pricing.ts](lib/pricing.ts). - Header gets the same badge pattern as ProfileBasicCard. **[app/pricing-profile/new/_components/AssignCustomersCard.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/AssignCustomersCard.tsx)** - Same shape. Editor footer: right-aligned **Mark as complete**, `disabled` when both selection arrays are empty. - Summary line: `${groupCount} group(s), ${customerCount} customer(s) assigned` (graceful pluralization). - Header badge mirrors the other cards. **[app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SaveAsDraftButton.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SaveAsDraftButton.tsx) → SaveAndPublishButton.tsx** - Rename file and exported component to `SaveAndPublishButton`. - Read `state` from `useProfileForm()` and add `const allComplete = state.basicCompleted && state.productsCompleted && state.customersCompleted` to the `disabled` prop. - Update button label: `pending ? "Publishing…" : "Save and Publish"`. - Toast success message likewise. **[app/pricing-profile/new/page.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/page.tsx)** - Remove the `Cancel` + `SaveAsDraftButton` pair from the header (keep the heading/description block on the left). - Below the cards container, add a footer row: ```tsx
``` ## Things to reuse (don't recreate) - The completion-flag-+-collapse pattern: copy verbatim from [ProfileBasicCard.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/ProfileBasicCard.tsx) (badge text + colors, Pencil icon, "Make Changes" outline button). - `formatAdjustment(adjustment)` from [lib/pricing.ts](lib/pricing.ts) for the products summary. - `useProfileForm()` / `useProfileFormActions()` from the provider — no new context needed. - `Button`, `Card`, `Badge`, `Link` primitives are all in place; no shadcn installs required. - Existing `useSaveProfile()` callback in the provider — the rename touches only the button wrapper, not the save logic. ## Out of scope - Validation beyond "non-empty selection" gates (no enforcement that adjustment values are sensible, no overlap-warning across profiles). - Auto-scroll between cards on mark-complete. The page already scrolls naturally; chasing the next card automatically is an upgrade for later. - Reverting on Cancel — the form state is component-local, so navigating away discards it without a confirm prompt. ## Verification 1. `pnpm tsc --noEmit` — type-clean (the new state fields and actions are all internal). 2. `pnpm build` — RSC boundary smoke test; the page tree shouldn't regress. 3. `pnpm dev`, navigate to `/pricing-profile/new`: - Header should NO LONGER show Cancel/Save. A new bottom action row should appear after the three cards. - Save and Publish should be disabled. Hover/inspect to confirm. - Fill in basic name → click **Mark as complete** → first card collapses, badge flips to "● Completed". Save button still disabled. - Select at least one product, set an adjustment → bottom-right of the products card has **Mark as complete** enabled → click it → card collapses to a one-line summary including product count and adjustment string. Save still disabled. - Tick at least one customer or group → bottom-right **Mark as complete** enabled → click → card collapses. **Save and Publish** now becomes enabled. - Click **Save and Publish** → button shows "Publishing…", toast appears, server reads the same payload as before. - For each completed card, click **Make Changes** → card re-expands with prior selections intact and the completion flag clears (Save and Publish disables again). - Click **Cancel** → routes to `/profiles`. 4. Hit `/profiles` and confirm the newly published profile appears in the list (the API contract didn't change).

# Add the ability to delete a saved profile

## Context

The Saved Profiles page ([app/profiles/page.tsx](app/profiles/page.tsx)) currently has no way to remove a profile once it's created — the only paths to the store mutate it via the "New profile" flow. The backend is already there: `DELETE /api/pricing-profiles/[id]` and `deleteProfile(id)` in the store both exist and work. This change wires up the UI: a trash-icon control on each card, a confirmation dialog, and a refresh of the list after deletion.

## Approach

A small client component (`DeleteProfileButton`) renders an icon-only ghost trash button in each card's `CardAction` slot. Clicking it opens an `AlertDialog` (newly installed via shadcn). Confirming calls the existing `DELETE` route, shows a sonner toast for success/failure, and runs `router.refresh()` so the server component re-fetches the profile list.

The page itself stays a server component — only the per-card action becomes a client island.

## Files to modify / create

1. **Install the dialog primitive**
   - Run `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add alert-dialog` (base-nova preset, same registry the existing primitives use).
   - This creates [components/ui/alert-dialog.tsx](components/ui/alert-dialog.tsx) and may add a small base-ui dialog dependency if not present.

2. **Create [app/profiles/_components/DeleteProfileButton.tsx](app/profiles/_components/DeleteProfileButton.tsx)** (new, `"use client"`):
   - Props: `{ profileId: string; profileName: string }`.
   - Uses `useTransition` + `fetch` + sonner toasts — same pattern as [app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SaveAsDraftButton.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SaveAsDraftButton.tsx).
   - Renders `<AlertDialogTrigger asChild>` wrapping a ghost-variant icon `Button` with `<Trash2>` from `lucide-react` and an `aria-label`.
   - Dialog body names the profile being deleted; confirm button uses `variant="destructive"`.
   - On confirm: `await fetch(\`/api/pricing-profiles/\${profileId}\`, { method: "DELETE" })` → on `res.ok` (204) call `router.refresh()` from `next/navigation` and `toast.success(...)`; on failure `toast.error(...)`.

3. **Edit [app/profiles/page.tsx](app/profiles/page.tsx)**:
   - Import `DeleteProfileButton` and `CardAction` from [components/ui/card.tsx](components/ui/card.tsx).
   - Inside each `<CardHeader>` (after `CardTitle`/`CardDescription`), add:
     ```tsx
     <CardAction>
       <DeleteProfileButton profileId={profile.id} profileName={profile.name} />
     </CardAction>
     ```
   - `CardAction` already has `col-start-2 row-span-2 self-start justify-self-end` styling, so it lands top-right without extra layout work.

## Things to reuse (don't recreate)

- `DELETE` handler: [app/api/pricing-profiles/[id]/route.ts](app/api/pricing-profiles/%5Bid%5D/route.ts) lines 35–40 (returns 204 / 404).
- `deleteProfile(id)`: [lib/db/store.ts:120](lib/db/store.ts#L120).
- Client-mutation pattern: [app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SaveAsDraftButton.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SaveAsDraftButton.tsx) — `useTransition` + `fetch` + sonner toasts.
- Toaster is already mounted (sonner exists at [components/ui/sonner.tsx](components/ui/sonner.tsx)).
- `Button` has the `destructive` variant available out of the box.
- `CardAction` slot is already styled for top-right placement in the card header grid.

## Out of scope

- Undo / soft-delete. The store hard-deletes by design.
- Bulk delete or multi-select.
- Cascade prompts ("this profile is applied to X customers — continue?") — the in-memory store doesn't track reverse relations, and the brief's resolver simply skips missing profiles.

## Verification

1. `pnpm tsc --noEmit` — type-clean.
2. `pnpm dev`, navigate to `/profiles`.
3. Seed data ships three profiles. For each:
   - Click the trash icon → dialog opens, names the right profile.
   - Cancel → dialog closes, card remains.
   - Confirm → toast appears, card disappears without a full page reload (router.refresh re-runs the server component).
4. After deleting all three, the empty state in [app/profiles/page.tsx:50-65](app/profiles/page.tsx#L50-L65) renders.
5. Restart the dev server (in-memory store resets via seed) and confirm initial state returns.
6. Edge case: hit `DELETE /api/pricing-profiles/does-not-exist` via curl → 404 with `{ error: "not found" }`. Trigger the same path from the UI by deleting twice in rapid succession (second call after refresh should be a no-op since the button is gone) — defensive `toast.error` shows on any non-2xx.

content.text:
All clean — type-check and build both pass.

**What changed:**
- [ProfileFormProvider.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/ProfileFormProvider.tsx) — added `productsCompleted` / `customersCompleted` state, matching reducer cases, and exposed `markProductsCompleted` / `markCustomersCompleted` actions.
- [SelectProductPricingCard.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SelectProductPricingCard.tsx) — collapses to a summary (`N products selected · ±$X.XX (Fixed/Dynamic)`) on Mark as complete; full editor returns on Make Changes. Replaced the Back/Next scroll buttons with a single bottom-right **Mark as complete** button, disabled while no products are selected.
- [AssignCustomersCard.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/AssignCustomersCard.tsx) — same pattern; summary shows group + customer counts. Mark as complete disabled when both selections are empty.
- [SaveAsDraftButton.tsx → SaveAndPublishButton.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/_components/SaveAndPublishButton.tsx) — renamed via `git mv`, gates `disabled` on all three `*Completed` flags, label is now "Save and Publish" / "Publishing…".
- [page.tsx](app/pricing-profile/new/page.tsx) — removed the Cancel/Save pair from the header; added a bordered action row below the cards with **Cancel** (links to `/profiles`) and **Save and Publish**.

I didn't manually exercise the browser flow. To verify end-to-end: `pnpm dev` → `/pricing-profile/new` → confirm the Save button stays disabled until all three cards show "● Completed", clicking it publishes and surfaces a toast, Cancel routes to `/profiles`, and Make Changes on a completed card restores prior selections.
are you happy with the solution you implemented?

Honest take — it works and matches the spec, but a few things I'd flag rather than claim to be fully happy with:

**Things I'm comfortable with:**
- The reducer extension and the `Mark as complete` → collapse pattern map cleanly onto the existing `basicCompleted` flow.
- Type-check and build are clean.
- Cancel/Save placement and the rename are straightforward.

**Things I'd want to look at again:**
1. **Disabled Save has no affordance.** A user who hasn't completed all three cards clicks Save, nothing happens, no hint why. A tooltip ("Complete all three sections to publish") or even just a hint line above the footer would close the loop. Right now it's a dead button.
2. **I didn't actually open the browser.** I only ran `tsc` and `build`. Both can pass while the collapsed-state UI looks awkward — e.g. the `CardContent` padding might feel off with just a small Make Changes button inside a rounded card. The plan's verification section is on you to run; I should have done at least one screenshot pass.
3. **I silently deleted the "skeleton-only" comment** in `AssignCustomersCard` (the note saying precedence-rule UX comes later). It was no longer accurate, but I should have called that out rather than removing it quietly.
4. **The products summary string is just okay** — `"5 products selected · -$5.00 (Fixed)"` is readable but a touch noisy. Something like a small chip row (Badge for adjustment + count) might fit the visual language better.
5. **`<CardContent className={completed ? undefined : "space-y-4"}>`** is a slightly hacky conditional. Not wrong, just not elegant.
6. **The form state lives only in component state.** Clicking Cancel discards everything with no confirm prompt. With the new "Save and Publish" framing (vs "Save as Draft"), users may expect a "you'll lose your work" guard. The plan flagged this as out-of-scope, but it's worth revisiting.

If you want, the highest-value follow-up is #1 (disabled-state tooltip) plus a quick browser pass to catch any visual jank from #2.

content.text:
Found it — same base-ui Select bug we hit before. The trigger's `SelectValue` shows the raw `id` ("bondi-cellars") instead of mapping to the customer's display name, because no render-prop is set. The dropdown looks right because each `SelectItem` renders `{c.name}` explicitly.

Fixing both Selects on the checkout page:

content.text: Type-clean. Both `Select` triggers in [CheckoutPreview.tsx](app/checkout/_components/CheckoutPreview.tsx) now use the children render-prop on `SelectValue` to map the id back to the human-readable name — so the trigger reads "Bondi Cellars" instead of "bondi-cellars", matching the dropdown items.
